column1: # mapping_set_title: CVE to CISA KEV Catalog LinkML schema mappings
#   Element-level mappings between the lmodel CVE schema (https://w3id.org/lmodel/cve)
#   and the lmodel CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog schema
#   (https://w3id.org/lmodel/kev-catalog). KEV is a thin enrichment layer that
#   identifies CVEs known to be exploited in the wild and adds remediation-action
#   metadata; mappings are mostly relatedMatch (KEV references CVE by ID rather

column7: A KEV entry references a CVE Record by cve_id and adds active-exploitation metadata; both extend Vulnerability from vulnerability_core but model different layers of evidence.
Inherited from vulnerability_core on both sides - same CVE identifier, same pattern.
Both label the vulnerability with a short human-readable name.
Both summarize the vulnerability; KEV's is constrained to a short blurb suitable for the catalog.
CVE date_published is the public disclosure date; KEV date_added is the date CISA added the CVE to the catalog (typically later).
KEV's vendor_project and product slots flatten what CVE models as a structured AffectedProduct.
